subject: a128ea355478cd378
findings: One Major, so REJECT (any Major = REJECT). MAJOR-1 (FIX 3, undisclosed, incomplete blast-radius): tightening the integrity pattern to [0-9a-f]{64} correctly flipped the 4 VALID vectors red (car fixed them) and added a proper 63-hex pinning vector, but left FOUR INVALID vectors (missing-kind, missing-basis, missing-abstract, missing-outcome) carrying short 61-hex placeholder integrity. Proven both directions: at parent 091efe6 restoring the intended field flips each Valid=True (isolated); at HEAD 0478afa restoring the field leaves Valid=False (masked by integrity length). These four negative vectors are the sole guards for the schema required array and its if/then conditional block (no positive vector catches a broken conditional); the harness only checks the boolean, so a future removal of a required-field rule would no longer fail them - the vacuous-guard the non-vacuity doctrine forbids. No false green today; the defect is a silent, undisclosed vacating of four guards. Mechanical fix: give those four vectors full 64-hex integrity. MINOR-1 (FIX 4): the wreckage header on canonicalise-demo.py cites 'Line 27, sweep = dict(hook)' but line 27 is 'def eid(obj):' and sweep=dict(hook) is line 46 - the 19-line header pushed it down and the citation was not updated (same-commit document self-invalidation); quoted code is inline so harm is bounded; fix 'Line 27' to 'Line 46'. FIX 1 and FIX 2 verified non-vacuous by fault injection (kinds.json corrupt -> parse assertion red at Schema.Tests.ps1:28; outcome+enum -> de-enum assertion red), both reverted byte-identical. FIX 4 diff is pure insertion (hunk -1,3 +1,22, zero deletions) and the header is otherwise accurate to the CLAUDE.md match-the-instrument scar. Point 6: NO escalation - Get-StarcarEnvelope drops any line before the first recognized key, so the car's extra-keys-before-outcome shape extracts Outcome and Findings cleanly (Found=True); issue #17's corruption class needs a key BETWEEN outcome and findings (reproduced separately) and is not this shape; all 9 store returned records have clean outcomes; the only residual (keys after abstract absorbed into abstract, non-misclassifying) is the already-tracked Envelope.psm1 continuation gap. CRITICAL PROCESS FINDING: the worktree, clean at my start, is being actively mutated during review by a concurrent Q3/Copilot-round-2 fix (Produce-Artifact.ps1 path-traversal sanitizer, +2 Producer tests taking the suite 88->90, .gitignore edit, staged .pyc deletion; mtimes 16:54-16:57 vs my 16:58, HEAD still 0478afa uncommitted) - two actors in one worktree, a dispatch-isolation hazard the conductor must reconcile before merging; my review of the frozen 0478afa stands and I left the dirty state untouched as read-only.
abstract: REJECT of PR-18 fix car at 0478afa. FIX 1 and FIX 2 are genuine and non-vacuous (fault-injected, reverted byte-identical); FIX 4's wreckage header is a pure top-of-file insertion, accurate to the scar except a stale line citation (Line 27 vs the real line 46, self-invalidated by its own header - MINOR-1). FIX 3 carries the one Major: the integrity {64} tightening was blast-radius-analysed only for the vectors that went red; four negative conformance vectors kept short placeholder integrity and are now double-invalid, silently losing the fault-isolation that made them the sole guards for the schema required and conditional-requirement rules - demonstrated by restoring each intended field and watching Valid flip True at the parent but stay False at HEAD. The suite is honestly green today, so this is latent, but it is an undisclosed vacating of four guards under the constitution's non-vacuity doctrine, hence REJECT. Point 6 clears: the live producer parser tolerates the car's full-schema envelope (extra keys before outcome are dropped, outcome/findings extract clean, all store outcomes clean) so issue #17 is not materializing. Separately and importantly, the worktree is being mutated live by a concurrent Q3 fix cycle during my review - the frozen-commit verdict holds but the conductor must resolve two-actors-one-worktree and the uncommitted unreviewed Q3 work before any merge. Recommend a same-car fix-cycle plus delta re-review for MAJOR-1 and MINOR-1.
